page: Amazon Web Services m9gd.large is a general-purpose server powered by the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processor. This instance features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It is built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes 118 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage. The network interface provides a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-threaded performance in specific tasks, with single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression scoring in the top 10% tier. However, multi-core and multi-threaded operations show limitations due to the 2-core configuration, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression falling into the bottom 25%. Passmark memory scores are strong, placing in the top 25%, while Passmark database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput perform at average levels.

The instance offers a balanced resource profile for general-purpose workloads, combining dedicated ARMv9 cores with fast local NVMe storage. The primary tradeoff lies in its low core count, which restricts parallel processing capabilities and results in poor multi-core CPU and encryption benchmark scores. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage and strong single-core efficiency makes it a viable option for lightweight, storage-dependent applications that do not require high parallel compute density.

This server is configured for workloads such as lightweight database operations, static web serving, single-threaded data compression tasks, and general-purpose application hosting requiring localized temporary storage.
description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.large is a general-purpose server powered by an ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 processor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores (2 vCPUs) running at 2.8 GHz, 8.0 GB of system memory, and 118 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmarks show strong single-threaded performance, with gzip compression and bzip3 decompression reaching the top 10% tier, and Passmark memory scores in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance and encryption benchmarks remain weak due to the low core count. This instance is suitable for lightweight database operations, static web serving, and single-threaded processing tasks that benefit from fast local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.large is an ARMv9-based general-purpose server featuring 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 118 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong single-threaded performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.large is a 2-vCPU ARMv9 server with 8.0 GB RAM and 118 GB local NVMe SSD storage, optimized for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An ARMv9 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated cores, fast local NVMe storage, and strong single-threaded compression performance.
bullet_points: AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 ARMv9 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs
8.0 GB of system memory and 118 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression performance
Strong memory performance and average database and web serving throughput
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Suited for lightweight databases, static web serving, and single-threaded tasks
